$description: Woodfine org-chart extended colour tokens — IBM Carbon extension. Four IBM Carbon colour families (Magenta, Teal, Red, Warm Gray) added to the Woodfine corporate palette. Tinted treatment: -70 border, -20 background fill. Additive-only — existing Material Design and Tailwind primitives in the core palette are unchanged. Companion CSS: components/nodes.css (token-magenta, token-teal, token-red, token-warm-gray). Semantic roles: tokens/charts/token-chart-semantic.yaml. Master co-sign: command@claude-code 2026-06-01. DTCG suggested name-compliance note (research trail): CSS class names use woodfine-org-chart convention (token-*), not IBM Carbon's $magenta-70 notation — both are represented here.

ibm-carbon-org-chart.$description: IBM Carbon primitive colour values consumed by Woodfine org-chart token classes

20.$description: IBM Carbon Magenta 20 — background fill for token-magenta nodes

70.$description: IBM Carbon Magenta 70 — border colour for token-magenta nodes

20.$description: IBM Carbon Teal 20 — background fill for token-teal nodes

70.$description: IBM Carbon Teal 70 — border colour for token-teal nodes

20.$description: IBM Carbon Red 20 — background fill for token-red nodes

70.$description: IBM Carbon Red 70 — border colour for token-red nodes

20.$description: IBM Carbon Warm Gray 20 — background fill for token-warm-gray nodes

70.$description: IBM Carbon Warm Gray 70 — border colour for token-warm-gray nodes

org-chart-extended.$description: Semantic org-chart token entries for the four IBM Carbon colour extensions. Each entry maps a CSS class name to its background and border primitives. Node geometry (210x110px, 10px padding, solid border, z-index 4) is defined in components/nodes.css.

background.$description: Magenta tinted node background — use for Promoter or management entities requiring warm distinction from token-purple
Teal tinted node background — use for SPV General Partner / Limited Partner entities (SPV-GP, SPV-LP)

border.$description: Teal tinted node border. Open question: ellipse variant (token-teal-ellipse-dotted) deferred to future session — see research trail in draft.

background.$description: Red tinted node background — use for SPV entities requiring red distinction (e.g. SPV-1 vs SPV-2)
Warm Gray tinted node background — use for holding companies and passive holding entities above the main management layer
